overview: This step modifies the size or format of image files. It takes a raw document containing an image and produces a modified raw document with the resized or reformatted image. Both source and target languages are required in the pipeline context.

scaleWidth.help: Sets the width of the output image as a percentage of the original width. Use `100` to keep the original width unchanged.
scaleWidth.values: Integer greater than 0. A value of `100` means no change; values below 100 shrink, above 100 enlarge.

scaleHeight.help: Sets the height of the output image as a percentage of the original height. Use `100` to keep the original height unchanged.
scaleHeight.values: Integer greater than 0. A value of `100` means no change; values below 100 shrink, above 100 enlarge.

makeGray.help: Converts the image to grayscale, replacing all colors with corresponding gray values.

format.help: Selects the output image format. Leave empty (or use the default) to output in the same format as the original. If a specific format is chosen, the file extension is **not** changed automatically.
notes: If the original format is not supported for output, a warning is generated and the output falls back to PNG if possible.
Selecting a specific format does **not** automatically change the file extension — you may need a separate renaming step.

limitations: Supported image formats on input vary depending on the Java VM, version, and platform.
When converting to a specific output format, the file extension is not changed automatically.
processingNotes: Width and height scaling are independent — setting different percentages will change the aspect ratio.
If the original image format is unsupported for output, the step falls back to PNG.

examples.title: Scale images to 50%
examples.description: Reduce both width and height to half the original size (the default configuration).

examples.title: Convert to grayscale PNG
examples.description: Convert images to grayscale and force PNG output format.
examples.config:
```yaml
scaleWidth: 100
scaleHeight: 100
format: png
makeGray: true
```
